ORDER GRANTING RESTAURANT DESIGN CONCEPTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-31-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT RESTAURANT DESIGN CONCEPTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN AND THE OPPOSING SIDE FAILED TO DISPUTE ANY MATERIAL FACTS. INSURER CANNOT MAINTAIN A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST ITS INSURED WHEN ITS EQUITABLE POSITION IS INFERIOR TO THAT OF THE INSURED. TRUCK INS. EXCHANGE V. COUNTY OF LA (2002) 95 CAL.APP.4TH 13. IT IS UNDISPUTED (FACT 11) THAT THE INSURED WAS COVERED FOR "PRODUCTS-COMPLETED OPERATIONS" AND OPPOSING PART FAILED TO PRESENT FACTS OR EVIDENCE THAT THIS COVERAGE DID NOT INCLUDE COVERAGE FOR LOSS SUFFERED BY PLAINTIFF'S OTHER INSURED, PRESIDIO. NATIONAL UNION FIRE INS. V. ENGINEERING-SCIENCE, INC. (1989) 884 F.2D 1208. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Defendant Restaurant Design Concepts, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Against Sentinel Insurance Company
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RESTAURANT DESIGN CONCEPTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY.

Appearances: Christopher J. Brennan of Bauman Loewe Witt & Maxwell, PLLC (503.446.3373) for plaintiff and responding party; Rebecca D. Takacs of Oles Morrison (510.903.2001) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and the opposing side failed to dispute any material facts. Insurer cannot maintain a cause of action against its insured when its equitable position is inferior to that of the insured. Truck Ins. Exchange v. County of LA (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 13. It is undisputed (Fact 11) that the insured was covered for "products-completed operations" and opposing part failed to present facts or evidence that this coverage did not include coverage for loss suffered by Plaintiff's other insured, Presidio. National Union Fire Ins. v. Engineering-Science, Inc. (1989) 884 F.2d 1208. Order signed. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
